sources.name: Kate Braestrup
sources.loss: Husband Drew killed suddenly in car accident while on duty as state trooper, left widowed with four children
sources.transformation: Months after death, felt 'aspect of me that wasn't being expressed', decided to pursue husband's dream of becoming minister/chaplain
sources.mission: Became chaplain to Maine Game Warden Service, helps families during search-and-rescue missions, provides spiritual comfort in wilderness tragedies
keyEmotionalMoments: Husband killed suddenly in car accident
Months after death, feeling 'aspect of me that wasn't being expressed'
Decision to go to seminary
Realizing the love and support she received could be given to others
Going down to the floor with grieving families
Finding purpose in husband's unfulfilled dream
Using humor to cope with darkness
keyQuotes: I go down to the floor with them and we stay on the floor and it's a very safe place to be
I know what I'm looking at when I see someone as they're being notified. And I am actually less unnerved by it than someone else
Sometimes the only way you can talk about something that's hard is to be funny

sources.name: Scarlett Lewis
sources.loss: Son Jesse, age 6, murdered at Sandy Hook Elementary School massacre, Jesse yelled 'Run!' to save classmates before being killed
sources.transformation: Returned home to choose warm clothes for funeral, discovered Jesse's message 'Nurturing Healing Love' on chalkboard, connected with Rwandan genocide survivors about forgiveness
sources.mission: Founded Jesse Lewis Choose Love Movement, created free social-emotional learning curriculum, reached 2+ million students in 100+ countries
keyEmotionalMoments: Son Jesse murdered at Sandy Hook
Choosing warm clothes for funeral ('didn't want him to be cold')
Finding 'Nurturing Healing Love' on chalkboard
Skype call with Rwandan genocide survivors
Jesse yelling 'Run!' to save classmates
Moment choosing to forgive
Realizing 'if Adam Lanza had felt nurturing, healing love, this would not have happened'
keyQuotes: 'Nurturing Healing Love,' Jesse had scrawled on the chalkboard
If Jesse could use the last moments of his life to save his friends, I certainly can get up every morning and do everything in my power
Hating Adam will not bring Jessie back. It will only hurt me and generate more anger and hatred
When we forgive someone, we do it for everyone. We do it for the whole world, and for our future

sources.name: Leslie Morissette
sources.loss: Son Graham diagnosed with leukemia at age 6, died at age 8 on Christmas Eve 1997, two years in and out of hospital
sources.transformation: During Graham's treatment, noticed sick children couldn't connect to outside world, remembered Graham's caring nature toward other sick kids
sources.mission: Founded Grahamtastic Connection, provides free computers/iPads/robots to sick children, helped 1,500+ children stay connected to school and friends
keyEmotionalMoments: Son diagnosed with leukemia at age 6
Two years watching son fight leukemia
Graham making other sick kids laugh
Christmas Eve death
Remembering Graham's caring spirit
Feeling Graham's 'angel wings fluttering' when helping children
Robots allowing kids to 'walk' down school hallways with friends
keyQuotes: Graham was such a special boy; he was just beyond his years. He always liked to help other people
Graham's special spirit is what kept me going
Every time I can help a child in need, I feel as though Graham is smiling down, his angel wings fluttering
I believe that Graham's love lives on in the work that I do

sources.name: Michelle Schwartzmier
sources.loss: Daughter Casey, age 20, died of accidental heroin overdose on January 15, 2017, overdosed next to packed suitcase for rehab she was supposed to leave for the next day
sources.transformation: About a week before death, Casey asked mother to write honest obituary about addiction struggles to help others feel less alone, fulfilled promise after death
sources.mission: Casey's obituary went viral, reached White House, receives messages from people in recovery, now speaks about addiction and breaking stigma
keyEmotionalMoments: Casey's request for honest obituary ('to help someone else feel less alone')
Overdosing next to packed suitcase for rehab
Dying in room where tea parties happened
Writing obituary that goes viral
Messages from people in recovery carrying obituary
The phrase 'She was supposed to bury me'
Receiving call from White House
keyQuotes: 'I would want to help someone else. To make them feel less alone. Even if it's just one person.'
Casey overdosed sitting next to her packed suitcase. She was supposed to leave for rehab the next day
In our home—in the same room where we had tea parties and bedtime stories—she overdosed
She was supposed to bury me
What started as a promise between a mother and daughter, turned into a legacy

struggle: Raw grief period with visceral details
perseverance: Making promises to honor the deceased
Realizing own experience can help others
breakthrough: Pivotal transformation moment (seminary decision, chalkboard message, son's spirit, daughter's request)
Decision to channel pain into service
Finding purpose in honoring the deceased
transformation: Ongoing mission helping others facing similar pain
Creating legacy that honors the deceased
Finding meaning and purpose in tragedy
Continuing bonds with deceased through service

bestMoments: Going down to floor with grieving families (Kate)
Fulfilling promise to write honest story (Michelle)
themes: Decision to help others facing similar pain
compositeElements.universalExperience: Transforming personal loss into purpose by helping others, finding meaning in tragedy, honoring the deceased through service

eslResonanceMultipliers: Communication & Language Barriers (metaphorical - grief as barrier, finding voice)
Learning & Education Journeys (going to seminary, learning from survivors, learning about needs)
Belonging & Identity (finding new identity through service, belonging to community)
Overcoming 'Not Good Enough' (overcoming guilt, self-blame, 'supposed to' narratives)
First-Time Courage (first time helping others, first time forgiving, first time speaking)
Building New Life (rebuilding after loss, creating new purpose)
Connection Across Differences (connecting with others facing loss, breaking stigma)
Persistence Despite Setbacks (continuing mission despite ongoing grief)
